skill.name: browser-automation
skill.description: Guides AI assistants through reliable browser automation with Playwright and Puppeteer. It focuses on selectors, waits, isolation, scraping workflows, and browser testing patterns.
skill.summary: Create reliable browser tests, scrapers, and AI browser workflows with Playwright and Puppeteer guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The four external command findings are false positives from Markdown inline code on SKILL.md line 70. The weak cryptography finding is also a false positive because SKILL.md line 3 is descriptive text, not crypto code. A medium warning remains because the skill recommends anti-detection and stealth browser automation techniques.

medium_findings.title: Dual-Use Anti-Detection Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill discusses detection systems and recommends stealth plugins for browser automation. This can support legitimate testing, but it can also help bypass bot defenses.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The wording directly references anti-detection patterns and stealth plugins. The skill contains no executable code, so the risk is dual-use guidance rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to Markdown inline code listing related skill names. No shell execution, script, or command invocation is present at this location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 70 contains only related skill names inside Markdown backticks. There is no executable Ruby code or shell interpolation context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography finding points to the front matter description. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function call, or encryption logic appears on that line.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is natural language metadata for the skill description. I found no evidence of cryptographic code in the scanned file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Stealth Browser Automation Advice
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill advises use of stealth plugins and anti-detection patterns. This pattern should be limited to authorized testing and compliant automation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The wording is explicit enough to justify a warning. The absence of runnable code lowers confidence that it creates direct operational risk.

content.value_statement: Browser scripts often fail because selectors, waits, and test state are fragile. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical browser automation patterns.
actual_capabilities: Recommends Playwright and Puppeteer patterns for browser automation tasks.
Explains stable locator strategies based on user-visible page elements.
Guides test isolation with fresh browser state for each test.
Highlights auto-wait practices to reduce flaky timing failures.
Identifies common browser automation anti-patterns and sharp edges.
Supports planning for scraping, UI testing, and agentic browser control.
limitations: It provides guidance only and does not include executable automation code.
It does not verify whether scraping targets allow automation.
It does not provide a complete testing framework or project scaffold.
Some anti-detection guidance can be inappropriate without clear authorization.

use_cases.title: Stabilize End-to-End Tests
use_cases.description: Use Playwright locator and auto-wait guidance to reduce flaky browser tests.

use_cases.title: Plan Web Scraping Workflows
use_cases.description: Design scraping flows that handle navigation, waits, popups, and changing page structure.

use_cases.title: Improve AI Browser Agents
use_cases.description: Give an AI coding assistant practical browser-control patterns for interactive web tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Framework
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a browser automation project
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose between Playwright and Puppeteer for my browser automation task. Explain the tradeoffs for testing, scraping, and maintenance.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Flaky Tests
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate debugging of unreliable UI tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my browser test approach and identify likely causes of flakiness. Focus on selectors, waits, shared state, and browser context isolation.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Scraping Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a browser automation workflow for collecting public page data. Include navigation, waiting strategy, popup handling, and rate considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Automation Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of a larger automation suite
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit my browser automation architecture for reliability risks. Evaluate framework choice, isolation model, trace strategy, locator design, and failure recovery.

output_examples.input: Help me reduce flakiness in my checkout tests.
output: Use user-facing locators for buttons and form fields.
Remove arbitrary fixed waits and rely on action-based waiting.
Create a fresh browser context for each test scenario.
Enable traces for failed runs so timing and page state are visible.

output_examples.input: Plan a browser scraper for public search results.
output: Define the pages, fields, and allowed access rules before implementation.
Use deterministic waits for result content and navigation completion.
Handle popups before triggering actions that open them.
Add rate controls and monitoring for failed page states.

best_practices: Prefer Playwright when cross-browser reliability and auto-wait behavior matter.
Use user-visible locators before CSS or XPath selectors.
Keep each test isolated with fresh state and clear setup.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on fixed timeouts as the main waiting strategy.
Do not share one browser context across unrelated tests.
Do not use stealth automation against systems without authorization.

faq.question: Does this skill run browser automation directly?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance for designing and debugging browser automation workflows.

faq.question: Which framework does it recommend first?
faq.answer: It generally recommends Playwright because of its cross-browser support and automatic waiting behavior.

faq.question: Can it help with Puppeteer projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers Puppeteer tradeoffs and notes cases where Puppeteer may be useful.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for web scraping?
faq.answer: Yes, but users must confirm that scraping is authorized and compliant with site rules.

faq.question: Does it include complete code examples?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a concise guidance document rather than a full code library.

faq.question: Why is there a medium security rating?
faq.answer: The skill mentions stealth and anti-detection automation, which can be misused without authorization.
